summary: A one-line stage decomp matching PR adjusted the stack padding in src/melee/gr/grinishie2.c for grInishie2_801FD4F0 from PAD_STACK(16) to PAD_STACK(12). The PR body says the prior pad placed a local Vec3 at 0x24(r1), while the adjusted pad moves it to the expected 0x20(r1), producing a 100% objdiff match. The bot report confirms one new match for main/melee/gr/grinishie2, grInishie2_801FD4F0, from 99.91% to 100.00%.

key_files.role: Only changed source file; contains grInishie2_801FD4F0 where PAD_STACK was reduced from 16 to 12.
evidence_refs: raw_diff.diff: hunk for void grInishie2_801FD4F0(Ground_GObj* gobj)
human_pr_text.md: PR summary describes PAD_STACK(16) -> PAD_STACK(12)

what_changed.text: In grInishie2_801FD4F0, PAD_STACK(16) was changed to PAD_STACK(12).
The PR body states the stack pad had been 4 bytes too large, and the change moved a local Vec3 from 0x24(r1) to 0x20(r1).
The PR body reports objdiff target=100.0 and current=100.0; the bot report lists one new match for grInishie2_801FD4F0 from 99.91% to 100.00%.

smart_moves.text: Kept the fix narrow: a single stack pad value was adjusted without broad source churn.
The author tied the PAD_STACK change to a specific stack-slot effect for the local Vec3 and supplied objdiff evidence.

decomp_lessons.lesson: For a nearly matched function with an otherwise plausible body, a small PAD_STACK adjustment can correct local stack-slot placement when objdiff shows a stack local is offset by the same amount.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md: stack pad was 4 bytes too large; Vec3 moved from 0x24(r1) to 0x20(r1)
caveats: This PR only supports the specific PAD_STACK adjustment for grInishie2_801FD4F0; it does not prove a general padding formula.
PAD_STACK remains a matching tactic requiring narrow verifier evidence under the loaded decomp standards.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use a narrow PAD_STACK value change to shift a local stack object to the expected r1 offset.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: Changing PAD_STACK(16) to PAD_STACK(12) was reported to move the local Vec3 from 0x24(r1) to 0x20(r1), completing the match.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Back stack-padding tactics with objdiff and bot/check report evidence.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: The PR body reports objdiff target=100.0/current=100.0 and the bot report records grInishie2_801FD4F0 as a new 100.00% match.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Narrow PAD_STACK adjustment can resolve a stack-local offset mismatch
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2643, grInishie2_801FD4F0 was matched by changing PAD_STACK(16) to PAD_STACK(12). The author reported the previous pad was 4 bytes too large, moving a local Vec3 from 0x24(r1) to the expected 0x20(r1), and objdiff reached target=100.0/current=100.0; the bot report confirmed a new 100.00% match.

source_update_candidates.title: Add PR #2643 as a concrete PAD_STACK evidence example
source_update_candidates.text: Possible example for matching-tactics documentation: PR #2643 matched grInishie2_801FD4F0 by reducing PAD_STACK(16) to PAD_STACK(12), with the stated effect of moving a local Vec3 from 0x24(r1) to 0x20(r1), verified by objdiff and a bot report. This should be framed as a narrow, evidence-backed tactic rather than normal style.
source_update_candidates.reason: The loaded standards already say PAD_STACK needs explicit evidence; this PR provides a concise historical example that may help future curators document acceptable evidence for such tactics.

rejection_notes: Do not generalize PAD_STACK(12) as a convention for grInishie2 or stage code; the evidence only covers grInishie2_801FD4F0.
Do not infer naming changes or semantic names from this PR; no names were changed.
Do not infer reviewer intent; no review comments were loaded.
